not less than 2 days before the date on which the application will be heard.


[ F21 (1A) Where the applicant is an individual, the application must not be served personally by the applicant himself or herself. ]


(2) The court may abridge the period specified in paragraph (1).


(3) [ F22 Subject to paragraph (7), service ] of the application must be effected by the court if the applicant [ F23 , acting in person, ] so requests (this does not affect the court's power to order substituted service).


(4) Where the application is served on the person who is the subject of the proceedings, it must be accompanied by a notice informing that person—


F24 (5) . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .


(6) Where an application is served by the applicant, the applicant must file a certificate of service stating the date and time of personal service [ F25 on each party served ] .


[ F26 (7) This rule is subject to rule 6.23A (which specifies how service is to be effected on a person where there are reasonable grounds to believe that person is residing in a refuge). ]


Textual Amendments


F18 Words in rule 11.4(1) substituted (6.4.2017) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2017 (S.I. 2017/413) , rules 1 , 4(2)(a)


F19 Word in rule 11.4(1) substituted (13.1.2025) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2024 (S.I. 2024/1016) , rules 1(3)(b) , 7(1)(a)(i)


F20 Words in rule 11.4(1) inserted (13.1.2025) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2024 (S.I. 2024/1016) , rules 1(3)(b) , 7(1)(a)(ii)

metadata.title: Part 11 — Applications under Part 4A of the Family Law Act 1996 or Part 1 of Schedule 2 to the Female Genital Mutilation Act 2003
